entries.name: Cormorant Garamond × Inter × JetBrains Mono
entries.notes: Inter as body is fine; only flagged when used as DISPLAY (the slop signal).

entries.name: Fraunces × Inter × JetBrains Mono
entries.notes: Fraunces SOFT axis at 100 turns playful fast; keep SOFT 0–30 for editorial gravitas.

entries.name: Playfair Display × Source Sans 3 × IBM Plex Mono
entries.notes: Playfair below 32px collapses on screen; use 40px+ only or thin strokes disappear.

entries.name: EB Garamond × Work Sans × Fira Code
entries.notes: EB Garamond's italics are stronger than its roman; lean italic for quotes and pull-outs.

entries.name: Crimson Pro × IBM Plex Sans × IBM Plex Mono
entries.notes: Crimson Pro at body sizes also works — consider single-family typography pairing.

entries.name: Lora × Nunito Sans × Cascadia Code
entries.notes: Nunito Sans can read juvenile at heavier weights; cap at 600 for serious surfaces.

entries.name: Domine × DM Sans × JetBrains Mono
entries.notes: Domine designed FOR body text — promoting to display works but loses identity above 48px.

entries.name: Spectral × Public Sans × Source Code Pro
entries.notes: Public Sans was designed for US gov — reads bureaucratic outside that context; pair with warm display.

entries.name: Source Serif Pro × Source Sans 3 × Source Code Pro
entries.notes: Adobe-designed superfamily; reads engineered/neutral — add personality elsewhere.

entries.name: Merriweather × Source Sans 3 × IBM Plex Mono
entries.notes: Merriweather is heavy on screen; cap at 700 unless going truly massive (96px+).

entries.name: Newsreader × Figtree × JetBrains Mono
entries.notes: Newsreader has optical sizing — must apply 'opsz' axis to match display vs body context.

entries.name: Libre Caslon Display × Libre Franklin × JetBrains Mono
entries.notes: Caslon Display only has one weight — locked into a single visual gesture; works on hero, less on cards.

entries.name: Young Serif × Inter × JetBrains Mono
entries.notes: Single-weight display means you cannot build a heading hierarchy from Young Serif alone — use size only.

entries.name: Instrument Serif × Inter × JetBrains Mono
entries.notes: Instrument Serif has become a Stripe/Linear-era cliché in 2025; still beautiful but signals 'I read TechCrunch.'

entries.name: Bricolage Grotesque × Inter × JetBrains Mono
entries.notes: Bricolage has width axis — fight the urge to animate width; treat as static type, not interactive toy.

entries.name: Syne × Inter × JetBrains Mono
entries.notes: Syne Mono exists — consider switching mono to Syne Mono for full-family pairing on art/gallery sites.

entries.name: Space Grotesk × Inter × Space Mono
entries.notes: Space Grotesk became the default 'web3 startup' font 2022–2025; design around the cliché or own it.

entries.name: Schibsted Grotesk × Inter × JetBrains Mono
entries.notes: Designed for Scandinavian news brand; carries journalism DNA — use for editorial, not pure marketing.

entries.name: IBM Plex Mono (mono-only)
entries.notes: Mono-only kills readability past two paragraphs — use for landing pages, dev tools, never long-form docs.

entries.name: Klein Condensed × Inter × JetBrains Mono
entries.notes: Big Shoulders shouts at sizes below 32px; reserve for 72px+ headlines only.

entries.name: Anton × Inter × JetBrains Mono
entries.notes: Anton was THE 2018-era Squarespace cliché; still works but feels 'designed by a marketer.'

entries.name: Archivo Black × Archivo × JetBrains Mono
entries.notes: Archivo Black is its own family — pair with regular Archivo for cohesion without contrast loss.

entries.name: Barlow Condensed × Barlow × JetBrains Mono
entries.notes: Barlow is the California DOT signage font — carries that institutional energy whether you want it or not.

entries.name: Outfit Display × Outfit × JetBrains Mono
entries.notes: Single-family typography saves loading but loses visual texture; works for product, fails for editorial.

entries.name: Manrope × Manrope × JetBrains Mono
entries.notes: Manrope-everywhere IS the AI-generated SaaS landing page look; pair with something idiosyncratic to escape.

entries.name: Geist × Geist × Geist Mono (Vercel)
entries.notes: This IS the Vercel look — instantly reads 'AI/dev startup'; deliberate choice or avoid entirely.

entries.name: Inter Tight × Inter × JetBrains Mono (Linear-style)
entries.notes: Inter Tight as display IS the Linear-clone signal of 2024–2026; differentiate or commit fully.

entries.name: Redaction × Inter × JetBrains Mono
entries.notes: Redaction has multiple decay levels (10, 20, 35, 50, 70, 100); pick ONE and stay there or chaos.

entries.name: Ogg × Inter × JetBrains Mono (premium display)
entries.notes: Ogg's italics are the headline asset; favor italic over roman for hero work.

entries.name: Tobias × Inter × JetBrains Mono (premium display)
entries.notes: Tobias hairlines vanish under 32px and below 1.5x device pixel ratio; size and resolution matter.

entries.name: Domaine Display × Inter × JetBrains Mono (premium display)
entries.notes: Domaine reads heritage-luxe; pair with too-modern body and the dissonance kills both.

entries.name: Founders Grotesk × Inter × JetBrains Mono (freemium)
entries.notes: Founders is THE design-agency default — choose deliberately, not by reflex.

entries.name: National 2 × Inter × JetBrains Mono (freemium)
entries.notes: National 2 reads warmer than Founders — better fit for brands wanting humanity not just precision.

entries.name: Söhne × Inter × JetBrains Mono (freemium, ChatGPT-style)
entries.notes: Söhne is the OpenAI/ChatGPT font — instant 'AI product' association; lean in or don't pick it.

entries.name: Pangram Sans × Inter × JetBrains Mono (Pangram Pangram)
entries.notes: PPP has become the 'design-aware founder' signal; signal-rich but no longer obscure in 2026.

entries.name: PP Neue Machina × Inter × JetBrains Mono (Pangram Pangram)
entries.notes: Neue Machina was the 2021 web3 cliche; less common in 2026, but still reads 'crypto-adjacent.'

entries.name: PP Editorial New × Inter × JetBrains Mono (Pangram Pangram)
entries.notes: Editorial New ultralight at 200 vanishes below 48px — sized below that defeats the purpose.

entries.name: PP Mori × Inter × JetBrains Mono (Pangram Pangram)
entries.notes: Mori is the 'we paid for our font' minimal-startup move; PPP licensing is reasonable but it's not free.

entries.name: Feijoa × Inter × Berkeley Mono (Klim premium)
entries.notes: Feijoa is calligraphic-warm and rare; Berkeley Mono adds engineering counterweight.

entries.name: National 2 × National 2 × Berkeley Mono (single-family premium)
entries.notes: Single-family pairings need careful weight discipline — display 700, body 400 minimum gap.

entries.name: Akkurat × Akkurat × Berkeley Mono (Lineto premium)
entries.notes: Akkurat is the 'we read Wallpaper magazine' typeface — earned by craft, not loud.

entries.name: GT Walsheim × GT Walsheim × JetBrains Mono (Grilli Type)
entries.notes: Walsheim was the 2018–2022 mid-market SaaS default; familiar enough to feel safe, not distinctive.

entries.name: GT America × GT America × JetBrains Mono (Grilli Type)
entries.notes: GT America has 6 widths × 7 weights — easy to overuse the variety; pick 3 weights max.

entries.name: Neue Haas Grotesk × Neue Haas × JetBrains Mono (Linotype)
entries.notes: Neue Haas is the 'real' Helvetica restoration — use Display for headlines, Text for body, never swap.

entries.name: Calibre × Calibre × JetBrains Mono (Klim)
entries.notes: Calibre was THE pre-Inter SaaS choice (Dropbox, etc.); now feels late-2010s if not balanced with distinctive display.

entries.name: JetBrains Mono everywhere (mono-only)
entries.notes: Works for dev tools and brutalist landing; readability collapses past 200 words — never long-form.

entries.name: Geist Mono everywhere (mono-only)
entries.notes: Mono-everywhere screams 'AI-platform 2024'; readable for nav and labels, never narrative paragraphs.

entries.name: Fira Code everywhere (mono-only)
entries.notes: Ligatures (== => -> :=) read as 'this is code' visually; disable for non-code surfaces.

entries.name: Berkeley Mono everywhere (mono-only, premium)
entries.notes: Berkeley Mono carries strong American-utility 1980s memory; doesn't suit fashion or wellness brands.

entries.name: Departure Mono everywhere (mono-only)
entries.notes: Single weight, pixel-style; works for memorial pages and y2k zines, fails on professional product surfaces.

entries.name: IBM Plex Sans Arabic × IBM Plex Sans × IBM Plex Mono (RTL)
entries.notes: Plex Arabic and Plex Latin are matched in metrics; line-height needs +0.1 in Arabic vs Latin context.

entries.name: Tajawal × Inter × JetBrains Mono (RTL)
entries.notes: Tajawal is heavily used in Saudi gov/startup; pairs naturally with Inter but reduce Arabic by 1 size step.

entries.name: Cairo × Inter × IBM Plex Mono (RTL)
entries.notes: Cairo's lighter weights vanish on Arabic glyphs; cap minimum weight at 500 for Arabic content.

entries.name: Amiri × Source Serif Pro × IBM Plex Mono (RTL editorial)
entries.notes: Amiri is naskh-style with deep classical roots; reads Quranic/scholarly — wrong for tech/startup, perfect for publishing.

entries.name: Noto Naskh Arabic × Inter × JetBrains Mono (RTL multilingual)
entries.notes: Noto is the Google 'no script left behind' fallback — neutral to a fault; lacks character of dedicated Arabic foundries.

entries.name: Rubik × Rubik × JetBrains Mono
entries.notes: Rubik has Arabic and Hebrew variants — great for tri-script products; slightly soft for serious B2B.

entries.name: Be Vietnam Pro × Be Vietnam × JetBrains Mono
entries.notes: Built for Vietnamese diacritics — best choice when shipping to that market; otherwise overkill.

entries.name: Playfair Display × Mulish × JetBrains Mono
entries.notes: Playfair + minimal sans is the wedding-blog / Substack / Etsy boutique cliché — overused since 2015.

entries.name: Literata × Source Sans 3 × JetBrains Mono
entries.notes: Literata was designed for Google Play Books — has optical sizing; ideal for reading apps and long-form.

entries.name: Epilogue × Epilogue × JetBrains Mono
entries.notes: Epilogue's wide letterforms swallow horizontal space; not ideal for dense data tables.

entries.name: Rubik Mono One × Inter × JetBrains Mono
entries.notes: Rubik Mono One is single-weight maximal; works as ONE word in hero, fails for headings beyond that.

entries.name: VT323 × Inter × VT323 (terminal cyberpunk)
entries.notes: VT323 is the 1970s DEC VT320 terminal screen; works as a deliberate retro signature, never neutral.

entries.name: Share Tech Mono × Inter × Share Tech Mono (cyberpunk)
entries.notes: Share Tech Mono is heavily themed — pair with neutral body or it's costume design, not type.

entries.name: Syne Mono × Syne × JetBrains Mono (art gallery)
entries.notes: Syne Mono carries the same designer's voice as Syne; rare unified-family triple for art and design brands.

entries.name: Tinos × Arimo × Cousine (system-safe metric clones)
entries.notes: Tinos/Arimo/Cousine are metric-compatible with Times/Arial/Courier — ideal for documents and legal printing.

entries.name: Alegreya × Alegreya Sans × JetBrains Mono
entries.notes: Alegreya's italic carries hand-cut energy; designed by Huerta in Argentina for long fiction reading.

entries.name: Petrona × Petrona × JetBrains Mono
entries.notes: Petrona has gentle terminals; good for wellness, slow-living, food brands — wrong for fintech.

entries.name: Abril Fatface × Poppins × JetBrains Mono
entries.notes: Abril Fatface + Poppins is THE 2017-era Squarespace template look — instantly dated and overused.

entries.name: UnifrakturMaguntia × Inter × JetBrains Mono (gothic)
entries.notes: Blackletter has carried fascist, gothic, and metal-band baggage; pick context with care.

entries.name: Marcellus × Lato × JetBrains Mono
entries.notes: Marcellus is built only for ALL-CAPS headlines; lowercase glyphs are functional but never the asset.
